nodes.text: Maya has been at her ThinkPad since one. She has not been doing anything productive. She has been scrolling, refreshing, scrolling again. She has been waiting for the message from F.T. that had, at 09:47, said: Diego is at the safehouse. I will tell you the address when it's safe to know. He is alive. Tell Sam in person — Ben is on it.
She has not, since that message, received another. She refreshes the chat window.

kid. one more thing. you said something to rick this week. you asked if we could help me, sometime, after this. i wanted to tell you i heard you. i wanted to say thank you for asking. i want to be honest with you. i don't know what 'help' would look like, for me. i have been in this warehouse for ten years and the warehouse and i have, in some ways, become the same thing. i don't know if i can leave. i don't know if i want to. i don't know if i should. but you asked. nobody has, in some time, asked. it meant something to me. i wanted you to know.
Maya reads it twice. She types:

I asked because I wanted to. If you ever decide you want to figure out what help looks like, I'm here. I'm sixteen. I'm not going anywhere for a while. I have a bike and a grandmother and a chat window. We can take our time.
She sends it. The reply comes in twelve seconds.
you are a daigle, kid. you are exactly the kind of daigle the good ones are. take your time. so will i.

now. one more thing. real one. there's going to be an issue with one of the adults this weekend. not your group. one of the adults. i can't tell you which one or what kind of issue. i don't actually know yet — the demons are flagging an anomaly but it isn't resolved. just — be careful with the adults this weekend. look at their faces when you talk to them. trust your read. — f.t.
Maya stares at the message. She types:
good kid. go take a nap.
Maya closes the laptop. She lies down on her bed. She stares at the ceiling. The pale yellow ceiling. She does not nap.
One of the adults is going to have an issue this weekend. F.T. doesn't know which. The Demons flagged an anomaly. I am supposed to look at faces and trust my read.

Which adults are in my life right now. My grandmother. Rick. Hal. Claire. Doyle. Detective Ramirez. Eileen Henderson. Chief Miller. Frasier himself, by extension. Mr. Henderson, who is fighting whatever's using him. And the people I haven't met yet — somewhere there is a Miriam I've heard about, somewhere there is, presumably, my father, whose status this week I have not been told.
Eleven adults. One of them is going to have an issue.
I need to pay attention to faces tomorrow.
She closes her eyes. She does, in the end, sleep — for forty minutes, on top of the bedspread, with her shoes still on.
